title: Ctenitis vellea
uses: Ctenitis vellea is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Himalayan region. taxonomy: Ctenitis vellea is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as the wood fern family. This species is a type of fern, a group of vascular plants characterized by the presence of a leaf-like frond. Ctenitis vellea is native to Southeast Asia and is often found growing in shaded, humid environments. The classification of Ctenitis vellea is further refined to the genus Ctenitis, which is a group of ferns within the family Dryopteridaceae.
